data.title: Keppel REIT obtains three loan facilities worth over $400m
data.description:
These include two $120m credit facilities and one $200m term loan facility.

Keppel REIT Fin. Company Pte. Ltd. has obtained three loan facilities with a combined value of $440m with each guaranteed by HSBC Institutional Trust Services.

Keppel secured two $120m credit facilities and one $200m term loan facility, it said in a bourse filing.

The REIT is required to prepay outstanding loans within five business days for the first $120m and 10 days for the second credit facility, the filing added. The $200m is required for prepayment within 10 business days.

data.title: NIO posts 15% sales increase in Q3 on competitive brand offerings
data.description:
The automaker delivered 87,071 vehicles, a 40.8% increase over the previous year.

NIO Inc.’s vehicle sales stood at $3.525m (RMB19.2m) in the third quarter, a 15% year-on-year increase, driven by competitive offerings of its NIO, ONVO, and FIREFLY brands.

NIO delivered 87,071 vehicles during the quarter, an increase of 40.8% compared to the same period in 2024, the automaker said in its unaudited Q32025 financial results.

Its NIO brand delivered 36,928 vehicles, whilst ONVO and FIREFLY delivered 37,656 and 12,487 during the period, respectively, it added.

The automaker said its gross profit reached $555,206 (RMB3.024m) or an increase of 50.7% over the comparable period.

It recorded an $646,455 (RMB3.521m) net loss from operations or a decrease of 32.8% quarter on quarter.

It also posted an $671,976 (RMB3.660m) net loss attributable to its ordinary shareholders or a 28.8% decrease QoQ.

For Q4, NIO aims to deliver 120,000 to 125,000 vehicles, or increases of 65.1% and 72.0% over the last quarter.

data.title: ComfortDelGro, Hello Robotaxi to roll out autonomous taxis in China, other markets
data.description:
The partnership will use ComfortDelGro’s fleet management and Hello Robotaxi’s vehicles.

ComfortDelGro Corporation Limited signed with Hello Robotaxi to collaborate on commercial robotaxi deployment in China and other overseas markets.

The collaboration will deploy and manage Hello Robotaxi vehicles through ComfortDelGro’s fleet management experience and physical assets, such as electric vehicle charging stations across its global network.

Hello Robotaxi, a China mobility technology firm backed by Alibaba, Ant Group and CATL, focuses on L4-level autonomous driving research and development and commercial application.

It will begin commercial operations in up to 10 cities, including its first overseas city, in a bid to scale its fleet to over 10,000 vehicles by 2026, according to a release.

The firm unveiled its first mass-produced robotaxi in September.

data.title: Coliwoo’s PATMI surges 62.9% to $22m in FY2025
data.description:
This is thanks to the demand for co-living business.

Coliwoo Holdings Limited posted a core profit after tax and minority interests of $22.920m in FY2025, representing a surge of 62.9% over the previous year.

This performance reflects continued demand for the co-living business, Coliwoo said in a release. It excluded non-operational items such as fair value adjustments and IPO listing expenses.

Coliwoo’s gross profit rose by 5.5% to $33.078m and its gross profit margin increased by 11% to 71% during the period.

The business’s net profit attributable to equity holders, however, declined by 51.4% to $15.047m, which also decreased the earning per share at the same rate to $4.82 per share.

It also saw increased income from rentals and management services. Its rental income from owned properties grew by 23.9% to $7.5m whilst rental income from leased properties increased by 4.9% to $32.4m.

Management services income saw growth of over 100% to $3.5m, which Coliwoo said was spurred by the onboarding of new properties and an improved average occupancy rate across its portfolio.

Coliwoo’s occupancy rate stood at 96.1% during FY2025. 

Coliwoo aims to grow its portfolio from 2,933 rooms as of end-September 2025 to nearly 4,000 rooms in Singapore by the end of 2026, it said in a release.

data.title: ThaiBev profit attributable to shareholders falls to $1.01b in FY 2025
data.description:
The company proposed $0.0248 total dividend despite the 6.8% profit drop.

Thai Beverage Public Company Limited said its net profit attributable to owners for FY2025 fell 6.8% year on year (YoY) to $1.014b (Baht 25,361m), whilst its overall net profit declined 11.7% YoY to $1,246.12m (Baht 31,153m).

The beverage giant attributed the drop in profitability to increased investment, including brand-building and new product launches within the spirits business, as well as higher operating expenses stemming from restaurant expansion in the food segment.

This was partially offset by the group's beer business, which achieved an impressive 24.6% increase in net profit. This was supported by stronger sales in Thailand and favorable raw material costs. However, this was offset by a decline in the net profit of the non-alcoholic beverage and food businesses.

Despite the overall profit decline, ThaiBev proposed a final dividend that brings the total dividend allocation for FY2025 to $0.0248 (Baht 0.62) per share. This proposal, made on 25 November 2025, is now subject to final shareholder approval.

(1 Baht = 0.040 SGD)

data.title: Seatrium secures contract for Tiber FPU project in Gulf of America
data.description:
The series‑build approach strengthens its foothold in Gulf of America projects.

Seatrium Limited has been awarded a contract by BP Exploration and Production Inc (bp) for the engineering, procurement, construction and onshore commissioning of the Tiber Floating Production Unit (FPU) in the Gulf of America. The award follows bp’s Kaskida FPU contract in December 2024.

The Tiber FPU will produce from the Tiber and Guadalupe fields in the Keathley Canyon area, about 300 miles southwest of New Orleans, in water depths of around 4,100 feet. It is designed with a production capacity of 80,000 barrels of crude oil per day.

Over 85% of the Tiber FPU design will replicate the Kaskida FPU, enabling Seatrium to apply established design efficiencies and lessons learned. The series‑build approach standardises procurement and project planning across both projects, while leveraging existing engineering partners and equipment suppliers.

The project adds to Seatrium’s portfolio of floating production units, which includes Shell’s Vito and Whale newbuilds in 2021 and 2023, and ongoing work on Shell’s Sparta and bp’s Kaskida FPUs.

data.title: Singtel Optus prices $200m fixed rate notes due 2035
data.description:
The notes will be converted to Australian dollars for Optus Finance’s medium term notes.

Singapore Telecommunications Limited (Singtel) said Singtel Optus, through its subsidiary Optus Finance Pay Pty Limited (Optus Finance), has priced $200m 10-year fixed rate notes.

The fixed rate notes, drawn under Optus Finance’s $4.5b (EUR3b) medium term notes, will be issued on 3 December 2025 and will mature on 3 December 2035, Singtel said in a bourse filing. 

The notes will be denominated in Singapore dollars and carry a coupon of 2.48% per annum.

The net proceeds from the issuance will be converted into Australian dollars and applied by Optus towards funding its business, Singtel added in the filing.

United Overseas Bank Limited was appointed as sole lead manager and bookrunner for the issuance.

data.title: NoonTalk Media secures shareholder backing amidst strategic content realignment
data.description:
Board continuity and micro‑drama pivot endorsed at FY2025 AGM

NoonTalk Media Limited received strong shareholder support at its annual general meeting on 31 October 2025, with all resolutions carried by margins above 99.8%, according to minutes released by the company.

Resolutions passed included the adoption of audited financial statements for the year ended 30 June 2025, directors’ fees of up to $120,000, and the re‑appointment of Foo Kon Tan LLP as auditors. Shareholders also approved the re‑election of Chairperson Dr Wee Keng Neo Lynda and Chief Operating Officer Zheng Xianbin, as well as authorities to issue shares and grant options under the NoonTalk Employee Share Option Scheme.

Chief Executive Officer Dasmond Koh reported on the organisation of the Golden Singa Awards, whilst Chief Operating Officer Zheng Xianbin outlined the company’s move into micro‑dramas. He noted the format’s lower production costs and shorter timelines, alongside rising demand in China and the region, as factors supporting the shift.

Shareholder questions focused on remuneration and accountability following recent losses. The Remuneration Committee, chaired by Cruz Teng, said executives are assessed against defined performance indicators and contractual deliverables, citing measures such as no‑pay leave and personal loans extended by the CEO to sustain operations.

data.title: Lion Global Investors launches physical gold fund as access expands
data.description:
The fund aims to track the LBMA Gold Price AM as closely as possible.

Lion Global Investors is launching the LionGlobal Singapore Physical Gold Fund, described as Singapore’s first insured physical gold fund with LBMA Good Delivery gold stored in Singapore.

The fund aims to track the LBMA Gold Price AM as closely as possible.

Standard Chartered Bank Singapore will serve as custodian, trustee, administrator, transfer agent, and gold provider. Distribution partners include OCBC, MariBank Singapore, Great Eastern, the Singlife Group, iFAST, GROW with Singlife, and dollarDEX.

Access will roll out in phases. OCBC and iFAST will offer the fund from 1 December. GROW with Singlife is running an initial subscription window from 18 to 28 November.

dollarDEX is set to onboard the product in December, whilst MariBank will add it through its app at a later date, with investments starting from $1. Great Eastern plans to make the fund available in 2026, including through ILP platforms.

data.title: CDL sells Bespoke Hotel Osaka Shinsaibashi as capital recycling advances
data.description:
The divestment follows CDL’s acquisition of the property in August 2023 for JPY 8.5b (around JPY 33.2m per key).

City Developments Limited (CDL) has agreed to sell the Bespoke Hotel Osaka Shinsaibashi to Blackstone for JPY 14.0b (about $117m), or JPY 54.7m per key.

Completion is expected in December 2025. CDL noted that the reference exchange rate for the transaction is JPY 100=$0.835.

The divestment follows CDL’s acquisition of the property in August 2023 for JPY 8.5b (around JPY 33.2m per key).

The freehold, 256-room hotel is located at 2-6-25 Minamisenba, Chuo-ku, Osaka, with a 938 sqm site area and 5,585 sqm gross floor area. It sits in the Shinsaibashi district near Midosuji, Parco, and Daimaru. CDL’s effective interest in the asset is 100%.

CDL said the transaction forms part of its ongoing capital recycling and portfolio optimisation strategy, bringing year-to-date contracted divestments to more than $1.8b.

Other disposals include interests in South Beach, Piccadilly Galleria, and a 250-unit multifamily property in Sunnyvale, California. Proceeds will be redeployed to maximise shareholder value.
